title: NOC 72024 – Supervisors, motor transport and other ground transit operators | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Supervisors of Motor Transport and Ground Transit (NOC 72024) are the operational engines of Canada’s logistics and urban mobility sectors. As the country grapples with a massive surge in e-commerce delivery and the expansion of light rail and subway systems in major hubs, these supervisors are critical to maintaining schedule integrity and safety. Classified as TEER 2, this role requires a blend of deep "road experience" and modern digital dispatching skills. In 2026, the focus has shifted heavily toward managing Electric Vehicle (EV) fleets and utilizing AI-driven route optimization to meet Canada’s new emissions and efficiency targets.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

duties: Operational Coordination: Supervising, coordinating, and scheduling the activities of truck, bus, and delivery drivers, as well as subway and transit operators.
Dispatch & Monitoring: Operating signal and track switch control panels for subway systems and dispatching bus drivers to ensure peak-hour schedules are met.
Performance Improvement: Resolving operational problems, recommending measures to improve performance, and requisitioning materials/supplies.
Safety & Training: Training staff in 2026-grade safety procedures, including Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) and specialized EV charging protocols.
Reporting: Preparing work reports, monitoring expenditures, and recommending personnel actions such as hiring and promotions.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Experience: Several years of experience as a driver or operator in motor transport or ground transit (e.g., Truck Driver, Bus Operator) are mandatory.
Skills: Proficiency in Fleet Management Systems (FMS), digital logbooks, and AI-assisted dispatching software.
Manual Dexterity: While mostly an office/control-room role, supervisors must often perform site inspections and field-coordinate emergency responses.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Good, with approximately 14,850 people employed in this sector and a steady stream of openings due to the "Logistics Boom":

Ontario (GTA & Regional): Very Good prospects. On March 25, 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72024 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker and In-Demand Skills streams. Minimum scores for regional draws (Southwestern and Northern Ontario) started at 53, reflecting an urgent need for transit leadership outside the Toronto core.

British Columbia: Strong demand. Particularly in the Lower Mainland for subway and light rail supervisors (TransLink) and truck fleet managers. BC projects a high volume of retirements over the 2024–2033 period.
Alberta: High Earning Potential. While demand is stable, Alberta offers the most competitive wages, with median rates reaching $48.50/hr in the Calgary-Edmonton transport corridor.

National Trend: Over 92% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time, significantly higher than the national average. The 2026 market favors those with experience in "Last-Mile Delivery" management.
Why Work in Canada as a Transit Supervisor?
Lead the EV Fleet Transition: Direct the implementation of Canada’s first generation of large-scale electric delivery vans and zero-emission municipal buses.
Strategic Supply Chain Role: Manage the "final mile" of the global supply chain, a sector that has grown by 22% in Canada since 2023.
High-Tier Stability: Enjoy a high rate of year-round employment (76%) and comprehensive non-wage benefits across most major transportation firms.
Priority for PR: In early 2026, this NOC was specifically highlighted in Express Entry Category-Based Draws for "Transport Occupations," offering a lower CRS threshold for eligible candidates.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On March 25, 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72024 with a score of 53+ under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.

2026 Express Entry (Transport Category): IRCC has prioritized "Transport Occupations" for category-based draws in 2026. Supervisors with 1 year of experience may receive invitations with significantly lower CRS scores.

2026 TR to PR Pathway: A one-time federal initiative launching in April 2026 targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Transit supervisors are classified as "High-Priority Essential Infrastructure Workers."
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for supervisors with a job offer from a BC-based logistics or transit firm, particularly in the Mainland/Southwest region.
